reasoning: The Democratic party retains an overwhelming advantage in California's 38th district. In 2024, Democratic incumbent Linda Sanchez won the district by 19.6 points. Although Sanchez is running in the 41st district in 2026 due to Proposition 50 redistricting, the 38th district remains solidly blue. Hilda Solis, a prominent and popular Democrat (former US Secretary of Labor and current LA County Supervisor), is running for the open CA-38 seat. Furthermore, Prop 50 was explicitly designed by Democrats to increase their partisan advantage across California, meaning they would not have redrawn CA-38 to be vulnerable to a GOP flip, particularly not when a heavyweight like Solis intends to run there. The market price for this seat is accurately pegged at 0.95. Short of an unforeseen scandal or catastrophic polling miss in heavily Democratic Los Angeles County areas, this seat will reliably stay in the Democratic column.
